title: Implications of rupture of the 2004 Sumatra-Andaman megathrust for the global subduction erosion archive and subduction zone structure
abstract: Subduction erosion is documented as the dominant tectonic process at approximately half of the world's convergent margins, including the Chilean margin north of ~33°S. A global compilation of convergent margin mass balance studies using seismic reflection data reveals that tectonic erosion removes material from the base of the overlying plate at a rate comparable to sediment accretion. The Chilean margin serves as a type example for the erosional mode of subduction.

location.description: Chilean margin (especially north of 33°S) as part of global subduction erosion compilation; uses CINCA and other legacy seismic data

seismic_lines.name: Compiled MCS profiles — North-Central Chile 18°–33°S (subduction erosion synthesis)

acquisition.vessel: Multiple vessels (compilation study; CINCA95 SO104 and legacy cruises)

data.name: Compiled MCS Profiles — Chilean Margin and Global (subduction erosion synthesis)
data.description: Compiled MCS reflection profiles from multiple cruises (CINCA95 and legacy) used in global subduction erosion mass balance synthesis; Chilean margin north of 33°S is key erosional example

_data_note: Geosphere paper; behind paywall. Global compilation using CINCA95 and legacy Chilean margin seismic data. Scholl and von Huene synthesis paper.

processing.summary: Global compilation of convergent margin mass balance studies using seismic reflection data documents subduction erosion as the dominant tectonic process at approximately half of world's convergent margins. The Chilean margin north of 33°S serves as a type example. Chilean data primarily from CINCA95 and legacy SONNE cruises.
workflow: 2. Sediment thickness measurement from MCS profiles
processing.notes: Geosphere review/synthesis paper by Scholl and von Huene; uses CINCA and other Chilean margin data as type examples for tectonic erosion

analysis_notes: Geosphere review paper; behind paywall. Synthesis using multiple Chilean margin datasets including CINCA95.
